title: audit — kill switches + rollout controls
description: Kill switches for audit capture + purge + export. All default ON. Turning capture off is EMERGENCY-ONLY — requires compliance-officer approval + a signed security audit row for the disablement.

flags.description: Master audit capture kill switch. Off = unbind the vendor AuditableObserver so subsequent mutations write no audit rows. Existing rows unchanged. EMERGENCY ONLY — requires compliance-officer approval + a signed security audit row. Used during migration incidents or catastrophic capture pipeline failures. Paired with the audit_capture entitlement for a two-key disablement (both must be off to actually stop capture).

PurgeExpiredAuditsJob kill switch. Off = the nightly job skips execution. Retention windows are still tracked; when the switch flips back on, purge catches up on the backlog. Used during regulator investigations that require freezing all purges cross-tenant until the investigation completes.
Export request kill switch. Off = POST /api/v1/audits/export returns 503. Used during platform-wide incidents affecting the S3 archive bucket or the notification pipeline.
ExpireRetentionOverridesJob kill switch. Off = policies do NOT expire even when override_ends_at elapses. Used when a legal counsel needs to freeze policy transitions during ongoing litigation.
ExportAuditsToArchiveJob kill switch. Off = hot-to-cold migration paused. Used during S3-side incidents or cost-management windows.

Raw-diff reveal capability. On = compliance-officer role may reveal old_values + new_values via ?include=raw_diff. Off = reveal returns 403 for everyone (including compliance officer). Emergency mode when a PII leak is suspected and reveal is temporarily locked.

Platform-wide enforcement of PII redaction in old_values + new_values (overrides tenant-level opt-in setting). On = redact for ALL tenants regardless of their setting. Off = each tenant honors its own setting.audit.redact_pii_in_old_new_values.

Enforces the MIGRATE-to-archive behavior on TenantErased (audit rows survive tenant erasure per §retention.json). Off = fall back to cascade-delete. NEVER turn off in production — turning off risks Art. 30 records-of-processing violation. Off allowed only in test envs to speed up test-tenant teardown.
